Mamma Mia! (2008)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $144,883,723Details
International Box Office $435,923,804
Worldwide Box Office $580,807,527
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $144,745,588
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $3,496,720
Total $148,242,308
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Donna, an independent hotelier in the Greek islands, is preparing for her daughter’s wedding with the help of two old friends. Meanwhile Sophie, the spirited bride, has a plan. She secretly invites three men from her mother’s past in hope of meeting her real father and having him escort her down the aisle on her big day.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$27,751,240 (19.2% of total gross)
Legs:5.22 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:24.9% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$52,000,000 (worldwide box office is 11.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,976 opening theaters/3,194 max. theaters, 8.1 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $227,034,961

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: July 18th, 2008 (Wide) by Universal
International Releases: July 2nd, 2008 (Wide) (Netherlands)
July 10th, 2008 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
August 8th, 2008 (Wide) (Czech Republic)
August 22nd, 2008 (Wide) (Argentina)
September 3rd, 2008 (Wide) (South Korea)
Video Release: December 16th, 2008 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for some sex-related comments.
Running Time: 108 minutes
Franchise: Mamma Mia
Plot point: Dysfunctional Family, Relationships Gone Wrong, Unexpected Families
Social setting: Wedding Day
Subgenre: Jukebox Musical
Source:Based on Musical or Opera
Genre:Musical
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Universal Pictures, Littlestar, Playtone
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Donna, an independent hotelier in the Greek islands, is preparing for her daughter’s wedding with the help of two old friends. Meanwhile Sophie, the spirited bride, has a plan. She secretly invites three men from her mother’s past in hope of meeting her real father and having him escort her down the aisle on her big day.
